text:
In a grove far, far away, there lay a garden bathed in twilight. Peacocks strutted their stuff, humming birds flitted about, and drowsy dragonflies skated through the dusk. At the heart of this enchanted enclave was an ancient tree whose hollow trunk housed a wise old owl.

The owl, Fluffywickles, had heard countless tales. Among them were those of a brave squirrel named Sammy who climbed the tree every day to feed its peculiar visitor. Everyone called Fluffywickles that, though they hardly knew why. The squirrel never talked.

One evening, though, something curious happened. While munching on acorns under the gentle glow of the moon, Sammy felt an unsettling silence. Then, he saw something small and peculiar descending from the bark above. To his utter astonishment, it was an old, feathered cloak, woven with glittering stars.

Curiosity piqued, Sammy rattled a bud and out spilled an antique feather.
